# Regression: the pre-commit hook (via GitAddDotSesam) must stage ONLY the
# nested .sesam tree, never unrelated worktree files. go-git's Add ignores its
# Path when All is set and would otherwise stage the whole worktree - which for
# a nested layout means sweeping in the surrounding project.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

mkdir sub
exec sesam init --user admin --sesam-dir sub
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Drift the nested secret so the hook has a reseal to stage, and drop an
# unrelated file at the worktree root (outside sub/, so sub/.gitignore does not
# cover it - a whole-worktree `git add` would pick it up).
cp newreadme sub/README.md
cp loose outside.txt

exec sesam --sesam-dir=sub hook pre-commit

# The hook staged the nested object it re-sealed…
exec git diff --cached --name-only
stdout 'sub/\.sesam/objects/README\.md\.sesam'
# …but must NOT have swept in the unrelated root file.
! stdout 'outside\.txt'

-- newreadme --
# changed readme
-- loose --
not a secret
